line.speaker: Class
line.dialogue: (chatter)

line.speaker: Nigel Ratburn
line.dialogue: Quiet down! Now as you may have heard - Buster, turn around, please - school pictures have just been scheduled...

line.speaker: Muffy
line.dialogue: What? Now? I can't have my picture taken in these rags!

line.speaker: Mr. Ratburn
line.dialogue: ...for next Thursday afternoon. That should give you plenty of time to change your clothes, Miss Crosswire.

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: There are a few things you have to remember about school pictures.

line.speaker: Flashback
line.dialogue: During the picture session Buster squirms on the stool.

narration.speaker: Arthur
narration.dialogue: Like, it's not a good idea to drink six cans of soda and then have your picture taken.

line.speaker: Flashback
line.dialogue: Prunella scrapes her feet on the rug a couple of times.

narration.speaker: Arthur
narration.dialogue: Or rub your feet across the carpet and sit in a metal chair.

line.speaker: Prunella
line.dialogue: Aah!

line.speaker: Flashback
line.dialogue: Binky sits in the stool with a flower boutonnière in his lapel.

narration.speaker: Arthur
narration.dialogue: Or wear a flower if you have allergies.

line.speaker: Binky
line.dialogue: Ah-tchoo!

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: One more out and we've got them!

line.speaker: Buster
line.dialogue: We'll never do it. They're fourth-graders.

line.speaker: Fourth-Graders
line.dialogue: (Come on!)

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: Remember the plan. No matter what, get the ball to Francine.

line.speaker: Third-Graders
line.dialogue: (C'mon Arthur! You can do it!)

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: Whoaaaahhh!

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: Watch it!

line.speaker: Buster
line.dialogue: I got it! I got it!

line.speaker: Third-Graders
line.dialogue: Aww...

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: Go! Go!

line.speaker: Third-Graders
line.dialogue: Come on! Go! You can do it!

line.speaker: Billy
line.dialogue: Ugh!

line.speaker: Buster
line.dialogue: Youuuuu're out!

scene.description: , Sue Ellen and Muffy are standing in front of the mirror.

line.speaker: Prunella
line.dialogue: My mother said I could get a new dress for picture day.

line.speaker: Muffy
line.dialogue: I'm getting new shoes and a dress.

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: We beat the fourth-graders! I tagged them and we won!

line.speaker: Prunella
line.dialogue: So, anyway, we should compare dresses and make sure we're not wearing the same thing.

line.speaker: Muffy
line.dialogue: What are you wearing?

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: To what?

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: Pictures? Who cares?

line.speaker: Prunella
line.dialogue: Not you obviously.

line.speaker: Muffy
line.dialogue: I think Francine will have the prettiest picture of all.

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: You do?

line.speaker: Muffy
line.dialogue: Except for mine, of course.

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: Ow!

line.speaker: Muffy
line.dialogue: It would take a lot of work, but... you could be a whole new Francine. We could even look like twins. Maybe.

line.speaker: Muffy
line.dialogue: Leave it to me.

scene.location:
her room Francine opens her closes and sports equipment falls out.
Francine I told you I didn't have any dresses.
Muffy What's that? Back there? (She takes out a pink dress and shows it to Francine.) Put it on!
Francine I'll look stupid!
Muffy Do you want a nice school picture or not? Trust me.
***
Francine looks at herself in the mirror wearing the pink dress over her normal clothes. Muffy examines the dress.
Muffy See? It's perfect. Once you fix the little rip over here. Just have your mom run a needle over that. And now

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: What's wrong with my hair?

line.speaker: Muffy
line.dialogue: A-hm! (She gets a brush. Francine sits on the toilet lid.) Close your eyes until I tell you to look.

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: But...

line.speaker: Muffy
line.dialogue: Do it! (Francine closes her eyes and Muffy brushes her hair.) Okay, you can look now.

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: Ahhh!

line.speaker: Muffy
line.dialogue: I don't get it. It always works for me.

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: Get me out of this!

line.speaker: Muffy
line.dialogue: Wait, I have an idea. (She takes out a cell phone and uses speed dial.) Hello? Flossie? This is Muffy.

line.speaker: Muffy
line.dialogue: Can I bring a friend to my appointment? She really needs something special.

line.speaker: Flossie
line.dialogue: You got it, kid.

line.speaker: Muffy
line.dialogue: Well that's great. Thank you. She hangs up. It's all set. Meet me tomorrow afternoon at the Salon de Beauté.

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: The what??

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: You didn't say we were going to a beauty parlor!

line.speaker: Muffy
line.dialogue: What'd you think it was? A pet shop?

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: No way am I going in there.

line.speaker: Muffy
line.dialogue: I knew you'd chicken out. Luckily, I brought my secret weapon. (She takes a photograph out of her handbag showing a muddy Francine with horrible hair and a goofy smile.) Remember this? Last year's picture? Is this what you wanna look like?

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: Okay... okay.... you win.

line.speaker: Flossie
line.dialogue: Muffy! How positively brilliant to see you! Everyone, look who's here!

line.speaker: Hair Stylists
line.dialogue: (Muffy! What a surprise! Fabulous!)

line.speaker: Flossie
line.dialogue: And what have we here?

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: Francine?

line.speaker: Flossie
line.dialogue: Oh, that's right. Ah, well, we don't promise miracles. Not in your price range anyway. (She puts Francine in a chair and takes a picture, which appears on a computer screen. The picture changes to Francine in pharaoh’s clothes.) This is our Queen of the Nile look, very popular. No? (Francine covers her eyes.) Well then, let's try for something more au courant.

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: Don't you have anything more normal?

line.speaker: Flossie
line.dialogue: If you insist. Ah, I have just the thing.

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: I'll take that, I guess. Only not too much hairspray.

line.speaker: Flossie
line.dialogue: You got it, kid. She speaks into a microphone. One prom queen to go, extra curls, hold the sauce!

line.speaker: Flossie
line.dialogue: (hums as she combs and cuts the hair, puts curlers in it and puts it under a hair dryer.) And now, ma chérie, you will rest and wake up as the girl of your dreams.

scene.location:
her dream she walks on a runway in a sports stadium. She is wearing a dress and her long flowing hair goes down to her feet.
Announcer Ladies and gentlemen
scene.description: , the moment you've all been waiting for. Let's hear it for Francine Frensky! Francine is sporting a brand new hairdo. The crowd is loving it.

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: Play ball!

line.speaker: Announcer
line.dialogue: She prepares herself for the opening pitch, as she takes her place behind the plate. And there's the pitch!

line.speaker: Fourth-Grade Bunny
line.dialogue: Huh? Ah!

line.speaker: Umpire
line.dialogue: Strrrr-ike!

line.speaker: Announcer
line.dialogue: What a dazzling play by Francine!

line.speaker: Announcer
line.dialogue: It's the bottom of the third inning, Francine runs all the way to the outfield and returns the ball... (Francine runs to the outfield, catches the ball with her hair and passes to Arthur.) ...to Arthur who makes the catch!

line.speaker: Umpire
line.dialogue: (removing his catchers' mask) You're out!

line.speaker: Announcer
line.dialogue: It's the final play of the game! Francine races to beat the ball, she slides, and...

line.speaker: Umpire
line.dialogue: Safe!

line.speaker: Announcer
line.dialogue: Her new hairdo touches the plate just in time! What a game!

line.speaker: Muffy
line.dialogue: Francine? Francine!

line.speaker: Muffy
line.dialogue: So, what do you think?

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: I knew it.

line.speaker: Flossy
line.dialogue: Oh, if only she had hair like yours.

line.speaker: Muffy
line.dialogue: I think it's great! Wait till you see how it looks with your dress!

line.speaker: Muffy
line.dialogue: Now, there are a few little things that you should know about hair care.

line.speaker: Muffy
line.dialogue: What are you doing??

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: Riding my bike!

line.speaker: Muffy
line.dialogue: I don't think so! Bicycles and curls do not mix!

line.speaker: Muffy
line.dialogue: See, I told you it was worth it! Now remember what I said; no swimming, no riding in convertibles, and wear protective headgear in the rain.

line.speaker: Mrs. Frensky
line.dialogue: Honey, Arthur called to remind you about the kickball game tomorrow. Don't forget to bring the ball!

line.speaker: Muffy
line.dialogue: Oh, I forgot. Absolutely no kickball!

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: No way!

line.speaker: Muffy
line.dialogue: And no lying down!

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: I can't do this! There's too many rules!

line.speaker: Muffy
line.dialogue: Remember my secret weapon? Well, then?

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: Okay.

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: Do you like it?

line.speaker: Buster
line.dialogue: No.

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: I wasn't asking you!

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: Well...it's....

line.speaker: Buster
line.dialogue: (ala Dr. Frankenstein) Aliiive! (makes a zombie-like face and pose)

line.speaker: Muffy
line.dialogue: Oh, pretend you don't see them! Boys ruin everything!

scene.description: , Francine and Sue Ellen sit at a table. Francine is about to bite into a sandwich.

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: (licks lips)

line.speaker: Buster
line.dialogue: Incoming! (Buster and Brain walk past Francine’s table) Was it an accident or does she look like that on purpose?

line.speaker: Brain
line.dialogue: Probably a combination of gravitational pull and too much stress on the hair follicles.

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: You are gonna get it!

line.speaker: Muffy
line.dialogue: Francine, calm down!

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: Ah!

line.speaker: Muffy
line.dialogue: Aah! Now look what you did!

scene.location:
the girls’ room Muffy cleans Francine’s dress with a paper towel. She puts a green bow tie over the remaining stain.
Muffy There

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: Yes you can! It's all over....

line.speaker: Muffy
line.dialogue: Ahem! Now, if you just stay still until picture time, everything will be fine.

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: What do you mean you can't play?!

line.speaker: Muffy
line.dialogue: You heard her! She can't mess up her hair!

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: Why?!

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: I have to keep it nice for school pictures.

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: But Francine, it's the fourth graders! They'll kill us if you don't play!

line.speaker: Muffy
line.dialogue: Good! No slouching. Remember, good posture is the key to true beauty.

line.speaker: Fourth-Grade Bunny
line.dialogue: Can I help you find the ball?!

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: Come on you guys, get up!!

line.speaker: Muffy
line.dialogue: Sit down!

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: It's one to nothing.

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: Two to nothing!

line.speaker: Muffy
line.dialogue: You have to get used to this Francine! If you want to look nice, no more kickball!

line.speaker: Prunella
line.dialogue: It's just a silly game anyway. What's the point of running around in a circle and getting your shoes dirty? (A ball comes flying towards the girls.) Aaaaah!

line.speaker: Buster
line.dialogue: She caught it and you're out!

line.speaker: Fourth-Grade Bunny
line.dialogue: She wasn't even playing!

line.speaker: Luke
line.dialogue: Did you know there's a poodle on your head?

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: Yeah, and it does tricks too! Watch!

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: grunts Safe!

scene.location: the auditorium the photographer is taking a picture of Jenna. Buster
scene.description: , Arthur, Francine, Muffy and Prunella are waiting in line. Francine’s dress and hair are in a mess. Muffy tries to fix the hair with a blow dryer.

line.speaker: Muffy
line.dialogue: How could you?!

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: I'm sick of being perfect! Besides, I like the way I used to look.

line.speaker: Photographer
line.dialogue: Next!

line.speaker: Buster
line.dialogue: Remember me? I'm the one who burped!

line.speaker: Photographer
line.dialogue: Next!

line.speaker: Muffy
line.dialogue: Listen to me! Don't go in there! You'll regret it forever!

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: You're making it worse!

line.speaker: Photographer
line.dialogue: Next!

line.speaker: Photographer
line.dialogue: Next!

line.speaker: Muffy
line.dialogue: Oh that Francine!

line.speaker: Muffy
line.dialogue: It's like my father says, "You get what you pay for!" And if I were you, I'd burn those and quick!

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: I sorta like them. See the smudge? I got it when I slid into home.

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: Hey, wanna trade?

line.speaker: Muffy
line.dialogue: Hers didn't come out. Technical difficulties.

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: I'll trade if you promise not to laugh!

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: I won't if you won't!

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: Okay.

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: I like it. Looks just like you!

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: So does yours.

line.speaker: Muffy
line.dialogue: I give up Francine Frensky! I'm sorry to have to say this, but you'll never be like me!

line.speaker: Arthur
line.dialogue: Kickball?

line.speaker: Francine
line.dialogue: You bet!
